title: Living in Boulder vlog 3 - 31 - 18: Catching up on stuff

enrichment.summary: Kyle films a casual vlog explaining why he didn't ride his bike today, focusing on sleep problems (body buzzing keeping him awake until sunrise), difficulty eating, asthma triggered by cycling, and his general effort to get caught up on life tasks like organizing computer files. He outlines plans to buy soups for easier eating, take sleeping pills, and study for a history test on the bike.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: needs to balance bike riding with other life tasks, not just ride constantly
body buzzing at night is a persistent problem; considering sleeping pills as a solution
struggling to eat due to salt sensitivity and appetite issues; plans to switch to hot soups for easier nutrition
considering quitting because it's worsening his lung/asthma issues
plans to study sober for two hours immediately before the test, possibly on the bike

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't ride my bike today because I'm trying to get caught up on my life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My goal is to not work on my bike until I get the wheels rolling.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Riding my bike is making me have a well-balanced life by forcing me to do other things.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been allowing time to get revenge on me by working all the time without much downtime.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last night, I rode my bike twice, totaling 30 miles, as exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent the rest of my time organizing a thousand-mile computer, which is a main task I need to catch up on.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I collect videos and photos but don't organize them, leading to a cluttered library.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tried to sleep early last night to ride today but was too awake and body buzzing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My body tends to buzz between 12 and 6 in the morning every day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ended up sleeping until 3 o'clock in the afternoon due to exhaustion from body buzzing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took melatonin and might start taking sleeping pills to regulate my sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Sleeping pills make me so tired that I can sleep through the morning, which is good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I swept my house and tried to have breakfast, which took two hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a big potato, a small bowl of macaroni and cheese, and made a bread patty with a scrambled egg inside, but didn't eat it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I opened a can of vegetables but couldn't eat it because it was too salty and tasted a bit rotten.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to go to the supermarket and buy all kinds of vegetable soups to make eating easier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to drink really hot soups to get food down quickly without taking much time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I need to quit weed because it's affecting my lungs, especially since I started riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might have exercise-induced asthma, which is worse when I'm in bed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I still need to organize files on my computer and find my video camera.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to study for my history test for two hours before taking it, possibly while riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I study sober for two hours before the test, I can make it an entertaining YouTube video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't ride today but still managed to talk for eight minutes, showing I'm just trying to get caught up with life.
